title: MIDBank (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for MIDBank in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is MIDBank?
faq.answer: MIDBank is an established Egyptian joint-stock commercial bank jointly owned by the governments of Egypt and the Islamic Republic of Iran. Founded originally as a project finance and investment bank, it has evolved into a full-service commercial bank offering retail, corporate, and investment banking services. With over 700 employees and a network of 16 branches across Egypt, MIDBank is positioning itself as a progressive force in Egypt's banking sector through its comprehensive digital transformation strategy.

faq.question: When was MIDBank founded?
faq.answer: MIDBank was founded on May 27, 1975, before the Islamic revolution in Iran, as an Egyptian joint stock company. Originally established as the Misr Iran Development Bank, it was created as a project finance and investment bank to facilitate financial cooperation between Egypt and Iran. Over its 50-year history, the bank has undergone significant evolution. In March 2021, MIDBank launched a new corporate identity under the slogan "Empowering Your Future," marking a strategic shift toward comprehensive development and growth.

faq.question: Where is MIDBank headquartered?
faq.answer: MIDBank is headquartered in Giza, Egypt, specifically at 21 Charles de Gaulle Avenue, Oula, Giza. The bank's main office is located at The Nile Tower in Orman, Giza. The headquarters serves as the central hub for the bank's operations across Egypt, housing key management and administrative functions.

faq.question: Where does MIDBank operate?
faq.answer: MIDBank operates through a network of 16 branches distributed across multiple regions in Egypt. The bank has a presence in Cairo, Giza, Alexandria, the Delta region, and Upper Egypt. This geographic distribution allows MIDBank to serve diverse communities throughout the country, from urban centers to regional markets, supporting the Central Bank of Egypt's efforts to boost financial inclusion across all Egyptian governorates.

faq.question: What services does MIDBank offer?
faq.answer: MIDBank offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services across retail, corporate, and investment banking segments. Retail services include banking accounts, certificates and deposits, savings accounts, Meeza MIDBank prepaid and debit cards, Classic and Gold Visa credit cards, salary and pension transfers, retail banking loans, and youth accounts. Corporate services encompass facilities and loans for small and medium enterprises within the framework of Central Bank initiatives, trade finance, treasury services, SWIFT transfers, and electronic clearing system transfers. Investment services include investment certificates, investment funds and securities, custodian banking and clearing services, and safe deposit boxes. Digital services have become a major focus, with the bank implementing Temenos Core Banking, Payments Hub, Financial Crime Mitigation systems, and Data Hub in April 2025. The bank is preparing to launch digital channels with Temenos Digital and exploring AI-driven innovations.

faq.question: What makes MIDBank unique?
faq.answer: MIDBank stands out for its bold digital transformation journey, becoming the first Egyptian bank to implement Arrangement Architecture (AA) and among the first in the MENA region to deploy the Temenos Data Hub. In April 2025, the bank executed a "big bang" approach to modernization, completely switching off its legacy in-house system and transitioning to Temenos' full-stack core banking platform in a single move. This comprehensive transformation included implementing Temenos Core, Payments Hub covering SWIFT, ACH, Instant Payment Networks, and Bulk Payments, as well as Financial Crime Mitigation systems. The bold approach has already delivered measurable benefits including reduced transaction processing times, improved operational efficiency, and enhanced customer satisfaction. Under the leadership of CEO Amr El-Garhy, MIDBank is positioning itself to lead the next wave of innovation in Egypt's banking sector, with plans to integrate AI capabilities and launch advanced digital banking channels.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is MIDBank?
faq.answer: MIDBank is a universal commercial bank operating under Egyptian banking regulations. While it was originally established as a project finance and investment bank, it has evolved into a full-service commercial bank offering a complete spectrum of banking services. As an Egyptian joint-stock company, MIDBank operates under the supervision of the Central Bank of Egypt and is licensed to provide retail banking, corporate banking, investment banking, and treasury services. The bank's operations align with Egypt Vision 2030 and the Central Bank of Egypt's digital agenda, reinforcing its role in advancing financial inclusion and sustainable economic development.

faq.question: What is MIDBank's approach to technology and innovation?
faq.answer: MIDBank has embraced a comprehensive technology modernization strategy that positions it among Egypt's most technologically advanced banks. The bank replaced decades of legacy systems with a modern Temenos banking platform in 2025, partnering with ITSS Global for implementation and DXC for testing. The transformation included the deployment of Temenos Transact (R21 Full Stack), making MIDBank a pioneer in implementing Arrangement Architecture for sophisticated product structuring and lifecycle management. The bank's technology stack now includes real-time data streaming capabilities through Temenos Data Hub, enabling advanced analytics and accelerated decision-making. Looking forward, MIDBank is exploring artificial intelligence applications through tools such as the Temenos Product Manager Copilot, with a dedicated team preparing to deploy AI-driven innovations in retail and SME operations. This positions the bank to bring new offerings to market faster and deliver cutting-edge customer experiences.
